methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Science Corporation is a Series B-stage biotech and health company founded by Max Hodak that has raised $200M+. The valuation analysis employs two applicable methods triangulated against a comparable universe of 8 private-stage peers and 3 public biotech companies.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $200M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $380M. This reflects typical step-up dynamics between Series B and subsequent funding rounds. The peer-median approach anchors to 5 same-sector, same-stage companies: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M). The median across this set is $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via EV/Sales multiples (median 5.8x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) is not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $323M to $931M, with a median of $595M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Science Corporation commands a discount to the peer median (closer to stage-progression levels) or sits within the peer distribution. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, clinical-stage progress, capital efficiency relative to peers, and founder track record premium in the frontier biotech segment.
